findings.message: The core blur requirement is internally inconsistent. This line says cards should use `backdrop-filter: blur`, but the same brief later correctly concludes that per-row blur should be avoided for performance and limited to hero/page-level panels. Analysis should inherit one clear rule, not both.

The theme-mode contract is muddled. The summary says the redesign should follow system preference or a user toggle, but non-goals makes a toggle explicitly out of scope and the intended outcome narrows to system-preference switching only.
The test-impact claim is too absolute. RTL tests cover behaviour only, but page/layout structure changes are in scope and no snapshot assertions prove zero test sensitivity.
Scope signal understates blast radius. Rationale says one CSS file + one layout + two page heroes, but NewIdeasInbox.tsx and InProgressInbox.tsx are also in-scope edit surfaces.
The forced-dark-theme diagnosis is well supported: .cmd-centre does hard-override the semantic surface/foreground tokens, and layout.tsx is its only usage site.
